summary: Validated Nexus production build and public deployment reachability. Local monorepo build/typecheck pass, but all public endpoint checks to 20.249.208.224 timed out.

critical.issue: Connection timeout from test environment; no HTTP response
Connection timeout from test environment; no HTTP response

ui_bugs.issue: Public URL not reachable (timeout), browser navigation failed
Could not verify expected 401 Basic auth because endpoint timed out

integration_issues.issue: All required public ports (80,3000,3001,3002,4000) timed out from this environment

action_items: Fix Azure NSG / cloud firewall / LB rules to allow inbound traffic for required public ports (or route via reverse proxy on 80/443).
After network access is fixed, rerun /app/tests/test_nexus_deployment_public.py to confirm web/docs/admin/api expected responses.
Provide/verify auth credentials file path: /app/memory/test_credentials.md (missing in this workspace).
Verify remote docker runtime with `docker compose ps` on server (not executable in this environment because docker CLI missing).
critical_code_review_comments: File /app/Nexus/apps/api-gateway/src/routes.ts is 1435 lines (>700 guideline); split into focused route modules for maintainability and safer production changes.
Admin middleware behavior is correct for production only when ADMIN_SECRET is present; otherwise it returns 503 by design. Ensure ADMIN_SECRET is always set in production deployment.
MOCKED: AWS S3 env values are placeholders; presign/upload flow will fail until real AWS credentials are configured.
MOCKED: @repo/webrtc-native package build/install is placeholder without native binding source.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Build/typecheck passed locally in /app/Nexus (turbo). Public reachability from this runner to 20.249.208.224 failed on all tested ports. A previous agent context mentioned server-local curl succeeded, indicating likely external network perimeter issue (NSG/firewall/LB) rather than app process crash.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: run pytest /app/tests/test_nexus_deployment_public.py -v. All tests fail with ConnectTimeout at socket connect stage (before HTTP handshake), including port 80 and app ports 3000/3001/3002/4000. This points to blocked inbound path from outside server network (cloud firewall/NSG/routing) rather than endpoint logic bug. Mitigation: open/forward ports properly or expose via reverse proxy on 80/443; then retest expected status codes (web 200, docs 200, admin 401 Basic, healthz/readyz 200).
